_dry_run_note: Dry-run manual executed by Claude playing the Investigator role. 6 web searches via WebSearch tool; direct site fetches blocked by session egress proxy (production Playwright will not have this restriction). Evidence drawn from search snippets only.

competitors.name: Napalm Rentals
competitors.positioning: Audiovisual equipment rental in Barcelona emphasizing competitive pricing, strong social media presence oriented to ad agencies and mid-range fiction

price_range.notes: Published as 'very competitive price' on public profile; no explicit figures retrieved from search snippets

competitors.content_style: Regular Instagram posts showcasing camera and lighting equipment (~5.2k followers)
observed_strengths: High social visibility relative to peers
Wide gear selection (ARRI + Canon + multiple mounts)
observed_weaknesses: No published pricing despite claiming competitive prices — friction for potential clients comparing options

sources.title: Napalm Rentals
sources.excerpt: Napalm provides audiovisual equipment rental in Barcelona with cameras, lenses in various mounts

sources.title: NAPALM RENTALS Instagram
sources.excerpt: @napalmrentals, 5,203 followers, camera and lighting equipment content

sources.title: Napalm Rentals | Barcelona Film Commission
sources.excerpt: C/ Pere IV 78, Bajos, 08005 Barcelona; 936 117 974; INFO@NAPALMRENTALS.COM

competitors.name: Zig Zag Rental
competitors.positioning: Comprehensive camera-department services (rental + technical support + transportation + workshops) targeting cinema and advertising professionals; founded 2013
competitors.content_style: Multi-platform visual portfolio (Vimeo for reels, Instagram for gear, Pinterest for inspiration)
observed_strengths: Broadest service wrap: not just rental but transport + workshops (education → loyalty)
Located at Calle Àlaba 51, Barcelona (22@ district, close to productoras)
observed_weaknesses: Open question: on-set technical presence not explicit in public materials

sources.title: Home - Zig Zag Rental
sources.excerpt: Founded 2013, comprehensive camera department services for film and TV

sources.title: Work Philosophy - Zig Zag Rental
sources.excerpt: Caters to professionals in cinema and advertising industries

competitors.name: Servicevision
competitors.positioning: Founded 1986 in Barcelona; designs and manufactures camera-support equipment (cranes, travelings, hot-heads, remote controls) in addition to rental; historically recognized as a top-tier cinema rental

price_range.notes: No public pricing; market-perceived as high-end (top-tier cinema productions)

competitors.content_style: Professional corporate tone, emphasis on engineering heritage
observed_strengths: Owns manufacturing (cranes, hot-heads, remote controls) — deep moat
Headquartered near El Prat Airport (logistics advantage for crews flying in)
observed_weaknesses: Low visibility on consumer social (Instagram, LinkedIn)
Site architecture split between .servicevision.es and rental.servicevision.es — UX friction

sources.title: Servicevision
sources.excerpt: Founded Barcelona 1986 by the Andrés and Alfredo Vallés brothers

sources.title: Cameras - ServiceVision Alquiler
sources.excerpt: Rental of equipment for film and television filming (Cameras, Optics, Lights, Cranes)

competitors.name: Camera Lenses Rental (CLR)
competitors.positioning: Camera & grip rental with presence in Barcelona and Madrid, specialized lens inventory (zoom, anamorphic, spherical, macro, prism) from 16mm to digital long format
observed_strengths: Lens depth (anamorphics + specialty glass) is a differentiator for DPs with artistic briefs
observed_weaknesses: Low social footprint compared to Napalm/Camaleón
HTTP (non-HTTPS) site — trust signal mildly degraded

sources.title: CLR - Camera Lenses Rental - Camera & Grip - Barcelona & Madrid
sources.excerpt: Equipment from 16mm to digital long format with variety of lenses including zooms, anamorphics, spherical, macro, and prisms

competitors.name: Ovide
competitors.positioning: Multi-city Spanish rental house (Barcelona, Madrid, Canarias, Euskadi, Sevilla, Valencia) with ARRI/Canon/RED/Sony inventory; geographic reach positioned as primary value

price_range.notes: Market perception (per Seito's ground truth): lower-priced end of the premium tier

observed_strengths: Only competitor in golden set with nation-wide presence
Suitable for productions that shoot across multiple Spanish regions
observed_weaknesses: Low social/content visibility (not appearing in social-focused searches)

sources.title: Cámaras profesionales para rodajes de cine en alquiler - Ovide
sources.excerpt: Rental of ARRI Alexa Mini LF cinema camera for films, series, advertising, documentaries

competitors.name: Camaleón Rental
competitors.positioning: Multi-country (Spain + Portugal) full-service rental, explicitly positioned on scale: '1,200+ projects in Barcelona', works with Netflix, HBO, Amazon Prime, Movistar+, TV3, TVE; targets international ad campaigns and streaming platforms
observed_strengths: Multi-city coverage (Madrid, Lisbon, Barcelona, Malaga, Seville, Majorca)
observed_weaknesses: Large-shop feel may alienate boutique productoras seeking personal service

sources.title: Film & Tv Camera Rental Barcelona - Camaleón Rental
sources.excerpt: Participated in 1,200+ audiovisual projects filmed in Barcelona for Netflix, HBO, Amazon Prime

price_summary: Published pricing is absent from all six major competitor sites reviewed — the market convention is 'request a quote'. Per Seito's industry knowledge (ground truth), the tier map is: Servicevision = premium, Camaleón = premium-international, Zig Zag = mid-high with service, Napalm = aggressive mid, Ovide = budget-premium multi-city, CLR = mid with lens specialty. The absence of published prices is itself a collective market weakness — any competitor that publishes transparent tiered pricing would create friction reduction for first-time clients.
channel_summary: Of the six analyzed, only Napalm and Zig Zag show meaningful consumer-social activity (Instagram primarily). Servicevision, Ovide, and CLR rely on web + industry directories (Barcelona Film Commission). None surfaced in searches with strong LinkedIn presence for B2B DP targeting. No competitor surfaced technical/educational content marketing (the 'authority' play). Vimeo is used only by Zig Zag among the six.

opportunities.description: Own the 'on-set technical authority' positioning on LinkedIn + Vimeo with short technical content aimed at DPs and gaffers
opportunities.rationale: None of the six competitors surfaced technical-educational content in search results, despite this being a direct value a rental house can add. This matches Seito's stated unique_strength ('hands-on technical support on-set')

evidence.title: Zig Zag Rental about-us
evidence.excerpt: Workshops for aspiring camera technicians — adjacent to but not the same as on-set client support

evidence.title: Servicevision overview
evidence.excerpt: Repair, transformation and target collimation workshops — internal engineering, not client-facing content

opportunities.description: Publish transparent daily-rate tiers for ARRI Mini / Mini LF — the single most friction-reducing move possible
opportunities.rationale: Every competitor hides price behind a quote form. A client comparing 5 rental houses with opaque pricing is a commodity bid. Transparent pricing signals confidence and saves them time.

evidence.title: Napalm Rentals homepage
evidence.excerpt: No public rates despite 'very competitive price' claim

opportunities.description: Boutique-personal positioning as counter-narrative to the large multi-city shops (Camaleón, Ovide, Servicevision)
opportunities.rationale: Three of the six competitors are explicitly scale-oriented (multi-city, streaming-platform logos). There is a clear opposite-slot for 'high-touch Barcelona specialist, one-on-one with DPs'.

risks: Camaleón Rental's platform partnerships (Netflix/HBO) represent a reputation moat that head-on competition cannot break short-term; avoid competing on scale.
Servicevision owns its own manufacturing — any 'tech superiority' claim must be framed around service, not gear.
Price-based competition with Napalm is a race to the bottom; the differentiator must be service quality, not rate.
open_questions: Actual published rates for ARRI Mini / Mini LF daily across all six competitors — blocked by session egress proxy, requires Playwright fetches.
LinkedIn presence and posting cadence of each competitor — requires LinkedIn lookup tool (not available in this dry-run).
Should Camaleón Rental, Movie Men, GV Broadcast, and Exodo Rental be promoted to Seito's golden competitor set? All surfaced multiple times in landscape searches but were not in the original 5.
Current reviews/sentiment for Napalm ('mal servei' per Seito's notes) — sentiment data needs targeted scrape of review sites.
